title: SD-JWT-based session token for off-chain verifiers

artifacts.description: Normative claim set spec: required claims (nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, agentMerkleRoot, exp, iat, iss, sub), optional selective-disclosure claims (permissions, delegatorRoot), SD-JWT serialization format, and binding to Bolyra handshake output.

Companion relayer contract that accepts a verified handshake proof, mints a signed session token reference (emits event with token hash + expiry), and exposes the registry signing key via EIP-712 typed-data or a dedicated getter. Does not store JWT on-chain — only anchors the issuance event.

issueSessionToken(handshakeResult, signingKey, opts): builds SD-JWT with mandatory claims from handshake output; verifySessionToken(token, registrySigningKey): validates signature, expiry, and claim binding; parseSessionToken(token): returns decoded claim set without verification.
SessionTokenClaims interface, SessionTokenOptions (ttl, selectiveDisclosureClaims, audience), and SessionTokenVerifyResult extending existing VerifyResult.
Unit tests: round-trip issue/verify, expired token rejection, tampered nullifierHash rejection, selective disclosure subset verification, missing mandatory claim rejection.
Hardhat integration tests: issuance event emitted after valid handshake, duplicate handshake replay blocked, signing key rotation reflected in getter.
How-to guide: integrate Bolyra session tokens into an API gateway or MCP server, JWKS endpoint setup, token refresh flow, and OAuth/OIDC bridge pattern.

steps: Define normative claim set in spec/sd-jwt-session-token.md: map nullifierHash → sub, scopeCommitment → scope, agentMerkleRoot → agent_root, exp/iat/iss per RFC 7519; specify SD-JWT disclosure list for permissions bits.
Add SessionTokenClaims and SessionTokenOptions types to sdk/src/types.ts, extending existing HandshakeResult and VerifyResult interfaces.

Implement issueSessionToken() in sdk/src/sessionToken.ts using a minimal SD-JWT library (sd-jwt-vc or @sd-jwt/core); bind claims directly from HandshakeResult fields; sign with provided EdDSA or ES256 key.
Implement verifySessionToken() in sdk/src/sessionToken.ts: verify SD-JWT signature against registrySigningKey, check exp, assert nullifierHash and scopeCommitment match expected values if provided.
Write SDK unit tests in sdk/test/sessionToken.test.ts covering all acceptance and rejection cases; run npm run test:circuits:fast to confirm no regression.

Design SessionTokenRelayer.sol: accept (humanProof, agentProof, nonce) same as IdentityRegistry.verifyHandshake(), emit SessionTokenIssued(nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, agentMerkleRoot, expiry, tokenHash); expose signingKeyHash() getter for on-chain anchoring.
Implement signing key registration in SessionTokenRelayer.sol with owner-only setSigningKey(bytes32 keyHash) and key rotation event; keep JWT signing off-chain, anchoring only the key hash.
Write Hardhat integration tests in contracts/test/SessionTokenRelayer.test.ts; run npm run test:contracts.
Add JWKS endpoint reference pattern to docs/session-token-offchain.md: how an API gateway fetches the signing public key from a companion HTTP endpoint backed by the relayer's signingKeyHash.
Export issueSessionToken, verifySessionToken, parseSessionToken, SessionTokenClaims from sdk/src/index.ts; bump sdk package version and update QUICKSTART.md with a session token example.
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md with a new section referencing the SD-JWT session token as the RECOMMENDED off-chain verification path.
dependencies: @sd-jwt/core or sd-jwt-vc npm package for SD-JWT serialization
HandshakeResult and VerifyResult types from sdk/src/types.ts
pot16.ptau and AgentPolicy/Delegation zkeys (no new circuit ceremony needed — session token is post-proof)
EdDSA or ES256 keypair for session token signing (operator-supplied, not a circuit artifact)
